headline: SpaceX stock gains, space companies fall, chips mixed on IPO news
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma joins Market Domination to tackle today's top trending tickers, including the momentum driving SpaceX (SPCX) shares in Friday's session after the company's IPO, the impact it is having on other space stocks Virgin Galactic (SPCE) and Rocket Lab (RKLB), and how semiconductor stocks such as Micron Technology (MU) and Nvidia (NVDA) are moving on the historic public offering.

headline: SpaceX Holds Most IPO Gains; It's Already The Sixth-Largest Company
summary: Shares of SpaceX traded around the middle of Friday's price range, as the most hyped IPO in years finally arrived in the broad stock market and immediately became one of the largest companies in the world.  The IPO raised $75 billion, making it the largest initial public offering in history.  It also values the company at roughly $1.8 trillion initially.

headline: SpaceX Opens at $150. That's Disappointing.
summary: SpaceX stock opened at $150, up 11% from the $135 IPO price and valuing the space company at about $1.9 trillion, according to Dow Jones Market Data. That makes Elon Musk's rocket company the sixth most valuable company in America, more than Broadcom and less than Amazon.

headline: HyperVerge, an AI-Powered Onboarding and Verification Platform, Incorporates Edgecore High-Bandwidth, Low-Latency Data Center Solutions for Increased Efficiency
summary: HSINCHU, Taiwan, June 12, 2026--Edgecore Networks has deployed high-capacity, open-networking switches for HyperVerge to handle AI training and IP storage workloads. Utilizing Broadcom hardware and the SONiC OS, the solution delivers strict latency control and allows HyperVerge to rapidly self-configure the system, demonstrating the speed and flexibility of open networking.

headline: What's at Stake for the Market
summary: Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen famously said that software was eating the world.  Now, AI is eating the stock market.  SpaceX, by virtue of its February merger with xAI, is the first of the money-losing frontier artificial intelligence companies to go public, beating OpenAI and Anthropic, albeit with a twist.

headline: Trump Pulls The Trigger Back, Nasdaq Rips 2.5% And SpaceX Books The Biggest IPO Ever
summary: Trump cancelled the planned strikes on Iran and the Nasdaq tore back 2.5%, chips led the rebound, oil dropped under $88, and the 10-year yield gave back 10 basis points in a session. Oracle kept bleeding on capex concerns. And in the last half hour, SpaceX priced the largest IPO in market history at $75 billion.
